description: This was a smallish imm/female hummer that came flying (first seen about 10 meters away) into my fuchsia t-shirt, hovering in front of it for 6-7 seconds and then flying off. As soon as I saw it coming toward me, I expected it to be a RTHU, due to the easterly CO location and the propitious month. The bird's back was bright green; the crown was grayish-green and separated from the whitish lower cheek and throat by a distinct -- but thin -- blackish mask; the underparts were whitish, except for a bit of buff on the sides/flanks; the tail lacked rufous, in fact being mostly dark, except for large white tips to, at least, the outer rects; the bill was of medium length, black, and straight (no downcurve at the tip). The tail was not wagged at all during the time it hovered in front of me.
